home.name: Adan Gonzalez Ceseña
home.title: Software Engineering and Emerging Technologies
home.description: Web developer focused on the Frontend, with full stack capabilities. I specialize in creating clean and responsive interfaces connected to robust back-end services.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Technologies i master
paragraphs: I studied Software Engineering and Emerging Technologies. My professional focus is on frontend and backend web development, using modern tools and technologies such as React, Vue, NestJS, TypeScript, among others.

I enjoy taking an existing design and turning it into a responsive and functional interface, taking care of both the frontend logic and the connection to the backend: token handling, SQL queries, authentication, etc. I also adapt to projects where I need to propose the design from scratch.
If you need someone to help you carry out your website, let's talk, I am always open to new projects and challenges.

items.title: Software Engineering and Emerging Technologies
items.description: Training in full-stack software development and emerging technologies, with hands-on experience in web projects, hackathons, and professional development at UABC.

items.title: Full Stack Developer
items.description: I led the development of a web platform for managing test/QA processes for projects within the Software Development Department (DDS) at UABC. I was responsible for the design, database, Keycloak authentication implementation, and both frontend and backend development. Initially, the team consisted of just myself and the client; approximately three months later, a teammate joined, who supported documentation and, in the final months, frontend development.

items.title: Frontend Developer
items.description: I participated in the frontend development of a web platform focused on supporting the development of skills in infants with developmental disorders. I translated Figma designs into functional interfaces in React with NextJs, maintaining constant communication with the project manager and proposing design improvements that were largely adopted. Everything was handled remotely.

modal.title: Project
modal.errorMessage: There was an error loading the project

items.title: Cibershield
items.description: An educational web platform focused on cybersecurity that offers information about threats such as phishing and malware
items.whatIdo: I carried out the complete redesign of this page. The page already existed; it was a project of my teammate, an old project with a lot of potential. However, my teammate and I concluded that a complete redesign was necessary due to feedback from some users. So while he handled the documentation, I carried out the full redesign and code to update the page
items.whatIs: CiberShield is an educational website focused on cybersecurity. We offer information about threats such as phishing and malware, and we have a fake email simulator: a section where examples of legitimate emails and phishing cases are presented, in which the user must read the content carefully and determine whether the email is legitimate or a phishing case

items.title: WolfForest
items.description: A website where you can improve your fictional characters to face them in a battle royale.
items.whatIdo: I carried out the full development process of this page: design, code, mechanics, etc. It is a redesign and refactoring of one of the first projects I made in my career
items.whatIs: It is a website where you can improve your fictional characters to face them in a battle royale. As you play, special events such as deaths, killings, temporary alliances, and object collection occur, creating a dynamic and random narrative.

items.title: Test Manager
items.description: This project is currently classified, but it was requested by the UABC Software Development Department.
items.whatIdo: I carried out the full development of the project: design, database, frontend/backend code, and supported deployment. However, I was not alone the entire time; at the beginning it was just me and the client, and after 3 months my teammate joined us and helped with documentation and, in the last months, with the frontend area
items.whatIs: This project is currently classified, but it was requested by the UABC Software Development Department. If you need more information, write to me and I will gladly talk about it

items.title: ActOne
items.description: A social network designed to share stories, where users can share their anecdotes in a format divided into acts.
items.whatIdo: I carried out everything involved in this project. It was a school project, but I really liked the final result; for now I am in the process of refactoring the code and the design
items.whatIs: A social network designed to share stories, where users can share their anecdotes in a format divided into acts. The platform also includes a concentration mode for content creators, allowing them to read the stories paragraph by paragraph, eliminating distractions and offering a more immersive experience.

items.title: ICPC
items.description: The ICPC (International Collegiate Programming Contest) is a university programming competition in which teams of three students solve algorithmic problems using a single computer for several hours. I participated alongside two teammates and we continue competing in different contest dates.

items.title: TalentLand
items.description: In 2025 I participated with university friends in the TalentLand hackathon at Expo Guadalajara, within the McDonald's track. We proposed an accessibility-focused application that would help users navigate the app through a bot and options adapted to different disabilities. Although we did not reach the finals, we were part of the 500 selected participants out of 1500 applicants.

items.title: NASA Space Apps Challenge
items.description: On October 4 and 5, 2025, I participated in the NASA Space Apps Challenge with my team. We developed a weather application idea that offers recommendations based on the user's plans and weather conditions. Although we did not win a place, the experience was very enriching.
